title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives and The Chats performed at House of Blues, with The Hives showcasing a mix of new and classic material.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist engaged the crowd with energetic antics and declarations of dedication to rock performance.
Opening act The Chats delivered a raw garage-punk performance, contrasting with The Hives' polished stagecraft.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer in 2023, McHugh continued running despite chemotherapy and surgeries.
McHugh runs to honor her late mentor, Susan Hurley, and as a testament to overcoming cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senators, including Ed Markey, expressed concerns about Meta's smart glasses potentially using facial recognition technology.
The lawmakers fear the technology could identify protesters, threatening privacy and discouraging public dissent.
Meta has been given until April 6 to respond about data collection and privacy safeguards for the technology.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy councilors delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan for new firefighter gear free of PFAS chemicals.
Current firefighting gear in Quincy tested positive for PFAS, linked to cancer, prompting calls for replacements.
Firefighters expressed disappointment over the delay, citing health risks from continued exposure to PFAS.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce costs and focus on conservation.
Capron Park Zoo faces $57 million in repairs and annual operating costs of $900,000, amid declining attendance and revenue.
Zoo veterinarian Bekah Weatherington criticizes the proposal, calling it a plan to close the zoo rather than transition.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown newlyweds Abigail Brown and Colin Wong adapted their wedding plans amid a blizzard for a microwedding in Somerville.
Meredith Goldstein explores the evolving language of relationships, particularly the term 'partner,' in her Love Letters newsletter.
Podcast hosts discuss the trend of women hiding male partners online, featuring Yowei Shaw's personal story of embarrassment.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback from Burlington, MA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor personal and family health journeys.
Averback's father was treated for glioblastoma at Massachusetts General Hospital and passed away in 2020; Adam survived testicular cancer in 2021.
Despite a recent serious blood infection, Averback is focusing on honoring his father and his own survival by completing the marathon.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard is working out with Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown, intensifying trade rumors.
New England reportedly made an offer for Brown, but Philadelphia declined; they're seeking a first-round pick in return.
Brown has consistently performed well, logging five straight 1,000-yard seasons and earning three Pro Bowl selections.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ends his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games, as BC loses to UConn.
Boston Bruins anticipate signing Hagens, potentially boosting their lineup during the final stretch of the regular season.
Hagens, a top prospect from the 2025 NHL Draft, is expected to play as a winger in his transition to the Bruins.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' features Bradley Cooper directing Will Arnett in a comedy-drama, now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl,' a new docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is streaming on HBO Max.
